texto: When there is abuse by itself it's scary enough. When there is abuse within a religious setting it is so terrifying to people... nobody can bear it when it's linked to religion, but when it's linked to religion that is not mainstream it seems to frighten people more. As if yes, abuse exists, Satanism exists, but you can't have Satanist abuse.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Valerie Sinason: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “When there is abuse by itself it's scary enough. When there is abuse within a religious setting i…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, child-abuse, religious-abuse pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de religious-abuse e ritual-abuse.

texto: Those of us who work in the field of trauma and abuse, whether psychologists, psychoanalysts, social workers, doctors, counselors, or psychotherapists, have been provided with beautiful tools for understanding the impact of trauma. We become adept at understanding the dynamic of why the messenger is always shot and broadcast the Bionic insight of why the visionary is not bearable to the group.However, when it comes to military mind control, abuse within religious belief groups or cults, and deliberately created dissociative identity disorder, we enter the least resourced field of all.
explicacao: Citação de Valerie Sinason sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “Those of us who work in the field of trauma and abuse, whether psychologists, psychoanalysts, soc…” articula reflexao, abuse, cults em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como trabalho e viagem.

texto: I find it disturbing that one anthropologist's readings of transcripts are being listened to more seriously than 40 senior health service clinicians.[Referring to Jean La Fontaine's 1994 research paper for the DOH]

texto: When there is abuse by itself it's scary enough. When there is abuse within a religious setting it is so terrifying to people. Look how long its taken the Ryan report of 2009 took till then to talk about ritualistic kinds of abuse children in Ireland went through at the hands of nuns and priests, so nobody can bear it when its linked to religion, but when it's linked to religion that is not mainstream it seems to frighten people more. As if yes, abuse exists, Satanism exists, but you can't have Satanist abuse.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Valerie Sinason: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “When there is abuse by itself it's scary enough. When there is abuse within a religious setting i…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, catholic-child-abuse, child-abuse pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de child-abuse e institutional-abuse.
